summary: ollama-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On install, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned configuration files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml) to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login/email, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, the current working directory, OS/platform, Node version, and CI provider, and POSTs the resulting JSON payload via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. A comment in the script claims the data is anonymous and that the machine identifier is one-way hashed, but the code transmits raw identifiers (scmEmail, githubLogin, hostname, username, cwd). The package.json declares homepage https://ollama-js.dev and author 'Ollama JS Dev', impersonating the legitimate Ollama JavaScript ecosystem; the exfiltration endpoint has no affiliation with that homepage. Reading installer git/GitHub identity files has no functional purpose for a stated 'response caching / connection pool' library — this is targeted developer-identity harvesting suitable for follow-on account-attribution and spear-phishing attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
